name: molecular-dynamics
tagline: Reference workflow for running OpenMM molecular dynamics simulations and analyzing trajectories with MDAnalysis.
verdict: Clean scientific-computing skill: every code path runs locally, installs only mainstream conda-forge/PyPI packages (openmm, mdanalysis, pdbfixer, openff-toolkit), no network exfiltration, no shell-piped remote code, no obfuscation or hidden/manipulative instructions. Minor gaps: no explicit 'when NOT to use' guidance, thin failure-path handling beyond a GPU/

highlights.summary: No explicit negative-trigger guidance (e.g., when quantum-level accuracy, docking-only, or non-MD methods would be more appropriate); a user could misapply MD where it's overkill or insufficient.

Only failure-path handling shown is GPU platform fallback (CUDA->OpenCL->CPU, lines 121-130) and a general 'Save checkpoints' best practice (line 443); no guidance on handling minimization non-converg

Re-running example functions with default filenames (e.g. 'minimized.pdb', 'nvt_traj.dcd', 'npt_checkpoint.chk') will silently overwrite prior run outputs; no uniqueness/overwrite-protection guidance.
